2604.20372.title: AI models of unstable flow exhibit hallucination
2604.20372.paper_summary: We report the first systematic evidence of hallucination in AI models of fluid dynamics, demonstrated in the canonical problem of hydrodynamically unstable transport known as viscous fingering. AI-based modeling of flow with instabilities remains challenging because rapidly evolving, multiscale fingering patterns are difficult to resolve accurately. We identify solutions that appear visually realistic yet are physically implausible, analogous to hallucinations in large language models. These hallucinations manifest as spurious fluid interfaces and reverse diffusion that violate conservation laws. We show that their origin lies in the spectral bias of AI models, which becomes dominant at high flow rates and viscosity contrasts. Guided by this insight, we introduce DeepFingers, a new framework for AI-driven fluid dynamics that enforces balanced learning across the full spectrum of spatial modes by combining the Fourier Neural Operator with a Deep Operator Network to predict the spatiotemporal evolution of viscous fingers. By conditioning on both time and viscosity contrast, DeepFingers learns mappings between successive concentration fields across regimes. The framework accurately captures tip splitting, finger merging, and channel formation while preserving global metrics of mixing. The results open a new research direction to investigate fundamental limitations in AI models of physical systems.
2604.20372.paper_summary_zh: 我們報告了流體動力學中 AI 模型的首次系統性幻覺證據，這在被稱為粘性指紋的水動力不穩定運輸的經典問題中得以展示。基於 AI 的流動建模面臨挑戰，因為快速演變的多尺度指紋模式難以準確解析。我們識別出看似視覺上真實但在物理上不合理的解，類似於大型語言模型中的幻覺。這些幻覺表現為虛假的流體界面和逆擴散，違反了守恆定律。我們展示了它們的起源在於 AI 模型的頻譜偏差，這在高流速和粘度對比下變得主導。根據這一見解，我們引入了 DeepFingers，一個新的 AI 驅動流體動力學框架，通過結合傅立葉神經運算子和深度運算網絡來預測粘性指的時空演變，強制在全範圍的空間模式中進行平衡學習。通過對時間和粘度對比進行條件化，DeepFingers 學習不同範疇之間的連續濃度場的映射。該框架準確捕捉到尖端分裂、指頭合併和通道形成，同時保留混合的全局指標。這些結果開啟了一個新的研究方向，以探討物理系統中 AI 模型的基本限制。
2604.20372.author: Ramdhan Wibawa et.al.
